hook.text: Two buildings, 500 meters apart. Running fiber costs thousands. A wireless bridge costs hundreds.

buildup.text: Outdoor bridges use directional antennas to create a point-to-point link between locations through air.

discovery.text: Modern bridges hit gigabit speeds over several kilometers using protocols optimized for outdoor use.

twist.text: Line of sight is required. Trees, buildings, or heavy rain can block or degrade the signal.

climax.text: Ubiquiti's airMAX and Cambium's ePMP have connected schools, farms, and villages worldwide.

punchline.text: Wireless bridges link buildings without digging. Cheap and fast.

quiz.question: What is required for an outdoor wireless bridge to work?
options: Clear line of sight between the two antennas
A fiber optic cable between the buildings
Both buildings must use the same ISP
